meta.name: DIY B2-W-style wheel-legged quadruped (approx half-scale)
meta.coordinateFrame: Right-handed body frame: X = forward, Y = left, Z = up. Origin at body geometric center. Legs mount near the four bottom corners. Left legs have +Y mount, right legs -Y mount; the hip-abduction (roll) axis is +X for left legs and -X for right legs so a positive commanded roll swings both sides outward (mirrored).
meta.units: millimetres (mm) for length, degrees (deg) for angles, grams (g) for mass, N*m for torque unless noted

body.note: Fore-aft X = 550, lateral Y = 340, vertical Z = 150. Monocoque/plated aluminium+carbon box; 'mass' is structure+panels only, actuators/electronics counted separately.

abduction.axisNote: Roll axis along body fore-aft X. Left legs use [1,0,0], right legs use [-1,0,0] for correct mirroring.
abduction.originNote: Equals the leg-mount coordinates (shown for FL); sign of x,y follow legMounts. Lightly loaded joint, smallest QDD module chosen.

hipPitch.role: hip pitch (thigh), swings upper leg fore-aft
hipPitch.axisNote: Pitch axis lateral (Y), parallel to knee axis.
hipPitch.originNote: Outboard lateral offset from roll axis to pitch axis (+Y for left, -Y for right); half-scale of B2 ~72 mm hip offset.

knee.role: knee pitch (calf), flexes lower leg one direction
knee.axisNote: Lateral (Y), parallel to hip-pitch. Knee bends one way only (asymmetric range).
knee.originNote: Down the thigh link (length 175) from hip-pitch pivot to knee pivot. Same module as hip-pitch for part commonality/spares/tuning.

thigh.note: hip-pitch pivot to knee pivot; equal to calf (1:1)

calf.note: knee pivot to wheel axle; equal to thigh (1:1)

wheel.tire: airless foam-filled/solid rubber, all-terrain tread, ~50 Shore A
wheel.axisNote: Wheel spins about lateral Y, same axis as knee output.

hubMotor.model: CubeMars AK60-6 V3.0 (QDD), coaxial with wheel
hubMotor.note: Peak drive need per wheel ~3-5 N*m; 9 N*m peak clears with margin. QDD chosen for torque-transparency/low unsprung mass; a purpose-built BLDC in-hub motor is the more elegant alternative.

compute.alternative: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Super Dev Kit (100 x 79 x 21 mm), current-gen recommended replacement (Nano B01 is EOL); same 69.6 x 45 mm SO-DIMM module footprint.
compute.note: Mounted on top deck, rear-of-center; heatsink faces up.

sensors.type: front RGB camera (Raspberry Pi Camera v2, IMX219)

sensors.note: inside body at geometric center, near CoM

sensors.type: depth camera (Intel RealSense D435i) - optional front
sensors.note: optional stereo depth; alt to or with Pi cam

battery.note: Mounted low and centered for low CoM.

massBudget.totalMassNote: ~13.15 kg; per-leg gravitational share ~3287 g (~32.3 N). Sits squarely in the ~12-13 kg wheel-legged class the actuator research targets.

standingPose.description: Symmetric squat with wheels directly under the four mounts (near-zero horizontal foot reach). Body center 360 mm above ground -> box bottom 285 mm off ground (within 250-320). Hip-pitch pivot 285 mm up, 140 mm wheel axle 70 mm up -> 215 mm vertical drop. With equal 175 mm links: 2 x 175 x cos(52 deg) = 215 mm. hipPitch = 52 deg thigh forward of straight-down, knee = -104 deg interior flexion (thigh and calf symmetric about vertical so horizontal components cancel). abduction = 0 keeps legs vertical in the frontal plane; positive roll leans/steers the body.

engineeringReview.note: Independent adversarial review of this spec. Fold these into any real build; they correct optimistic entries above.
engineeringReview.bodyMass: The 1500 g body assumes a skeletal frame + thin skins, NOT a solid 4 mm aluminium/carbon box (that would weigh ~4-7 kg). If you build a solid 4 mm monocoque, budget the body at 3-5 kg and expect total ~16-18 kg, which raises every gravitational torque ~25-35%. Decide construction before final motor sizing.
engineeringReview.hipKneeTorque: Hip-pitch AND knee are equally the most-loaded joints in a symmetric squat. GO-M8010-6 (9.5 N*m rated / 23.7 N*m peak) is adequate for rolling and gentle stepping IF commanded horizontal foot reach is capped to ~100-120 mm (holding torque stays under ~7-8 N*m, within continuous). It is NOT sufficient for jumping / hard landings / fully-extended-leg loads, especially at the corrected higher mass. For jump dynamics upgrade hip+knee to e.g. CubeMars AK70-10 (~24.8 N*m peak / 8.3 N*m continuous).
engineeringReview.abductionTorque: Abduction (DM-J4310-2EC, 3 N*m rated / 7 N*m peak) is the tightest joint. Fine with legs near-vertical; load-bearing at large splay (>~30 deg) can exceed peak. Limit weighted stance to small roll, or upsize (e.g. DM-J4340) for lean-steering under load.
engineeringReview.swingInertia: Account for swing-leg inertial torque: 730 g at the toe (wheel 350 g + AK60-6 380 g) adds dynamic hip/knee torque during fast recirculation. A lighter in-hub BLDC would cut unsprung mass.
engineeringReview.batteryMass: Budget the 6S 8 Ah LiPo at ~1100 g (not 900 g).
engineeringReview.abductionMotorDims: Confirm DM-J4310-2EC true outer diameter from the datasheet before finalizing the hip-corner packaging where three actuators cluster (spec lists 57 x 46 mm).
engineeringReview.faithfulWheel: A strict half-scale B2-W wheel would be ~112 mm; this spec uses 140 mm (bigger, better obstacle rolling). Either is acceptable.
engineeringReview.fusionScript: build_robot.py revolute joints were corrected to use createForAssemblyContext proxies for nested occurrences, and the root rename is guarded so it cannot abort the build.
